contaminant.name: Brake Pad Dust Deposits
contaminant.description: Brake-dust contamination, it emerges from frictional wear of steel, aluminum, and cast iron during braking processes, forming adherent particulate layers that accumulate on surfaces. This residue, distinguished by its metallic tenacity and uneven distribution—which resists dislodgement more than organic dusts—behaves variably: on cast iron it embeds deeply, dependent from surface porosity, while on aluminum it manifests as thinner films, posing challenges like potential scratching in mechanical removal. Laser cleaning addresses these effectively, as the process vaporizes the particles selectively through targeted ablation, which preserves substrate integrity without residue recurrence.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Zinc Oxide, Carbon Black, Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation. WARNING: Carbon Black, Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
